zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine"於1073年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-達瓦波奧蒙吉素爾-瓦時，她的父親Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont34歲，母親Adélaide de Gournay24歲。她於1090年之前與Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency"在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-達瓦蒙特莫倫希結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1124年在法國i萊-德-福朗斯蒙特莫倫希享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine"于1073年出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-達瓦波奧蒙吉素爾-瓦时，她的父亲Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont34岁，母亲Adélaide de Gournay24岁。她于1090年之前与Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency"在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-達瓦蒙特莫倫希结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1124年在法国i萊-德-福朗斯蒙特莫倫希享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: "Dame
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: from Middle English, Old French dame ‘lady’ (from Latin domina ‘mistress’), applied as a nickname for a foppish young man whose outfit and manner have an effeminate elegance, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a lady. As a French name it may also be a nickname meaning ‘mother-in-law’.
English: possibly an East Anglian variant of   Dam   4.

variants.name: Dam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Hoa, Tuan, Ngoc, Thanh, Binh, Chanh, Duc, Hiep, Minh, Nu, Tien.  Southeast Asian Hong, Chan, Dong, Hon, Man, Oi, Ok, Phoung, Sang, Sokhom, Sun.
Vietnamese (Đàm): from the Chinese surnames 譚, 谈 and 覃, see Tan 1-3.
Cambodian: written ដាំ, possibly of Vietnamese origin (see above). The surname ដាំ also corresponds to the Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘to plant’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine" was born in 1073, in Beaumont-sur-Oise, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, France, her father, Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont, was 34 and her mother, Adélaide de Gournay, was 24. She married Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency" before 1090, in Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1124, in Montmorency, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine" est née en 1073, à Beaumont-sur-Oise, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, France, son père, Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Adélaide de Gournay, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency" avant 1090, à Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1124, à Montmorency, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine" 1073 in Beaumont-sur-Oise, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont 34 und ihre Mutter Adélaide de Gournay 24. Sie hat vor 1090 in Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, Frankreich Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency" geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1124 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Montmorency, Île-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine" è nata il nel 1073 nel Beaumont-sur-Oise, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Adélaide de Gournay, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency" prima del 1090, nel Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1124, nel Montmorency, Île-de-France, Francia, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine"が1073年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスヴァル-ド'オイゼビーウモント-スル-オイゼで生まれた時，父Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumontは34歳，母Adélaide de Gournayは24歳であった。1090年以前にフランスiレ-デ-フランスヴァル-ド'オイゼモントモーエンシでBouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency"と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1124年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスモントモーエンシで52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine"(이)가 1073년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 발-드'이스 보몽-수-이스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adélaide de Gournay(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1090년 이전 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 발-드'이스 몽모렌시에서 Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency"(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1124년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 몽모렌시에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine" nasceu em 1073, em Beaumont-sur-Oise, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Adélaide de Gournay, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency" antes 1090, em Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1124, em Montmorency, Île-de-France, França, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine" родилась в 1073 г. в местности бомонт-сур-оис, вал-д'оис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Adélaide de Gournay, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency" перед 1090 г. в местности монтморенси, вал-д'оис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1124 г. в местности монтморенси, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes de Beaumont, "Dame de Conflans Sainte-Honorine" nació en 1073, en Beaumont-sur-Oise, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Ives III Count Beaumont-Sur-Oise de Beaumont, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Adélaide de Gournay, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Bouchard IV de Montmorency "Seigneur de Montmorency" antes de 1090, en Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1124, en Montmorency, Île-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 52 años.
